element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Object manipulation and enemy stunning used throughout early levels
Environmental puzzle solving and fuse lighting introduced gradually
Character-specific progression gates requiring kiss triggers

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy variety (Pinky and the Brain's robots)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Increasing complexity and combinations in later levels
Escalating difficulty through Levels 2-5; final boss rush in Level 5
Accumulation mechanic driving progression through all five levels

element_introduction_order.element: Stars and golden face pickups (life extension)
Final confrontation with Pinky and the Brain
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culmination of all character abilities and survival skills

typical_tension_triggers: Difficult platforming sections requiring character abilities
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate tension with cyclical boss rush pattern; four freely-ordered mid-game levels provide player agency over difficulty curve, followed by mandatory final gauntlet after fifth level unlock

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Movie memorabilia placement; health pickups scattered as waypoints

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of character abilities and basic enemy patterns across four optional levels; player can sequence difficulty based on level selection order
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Four main levels with increasing enemy complexity and environmental puzzle integration; flexibility in progression order allows difficulty customization
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fifth level mandatory after four memorabilia collected; boss rush format combining all previously encountered bosses; final Pinky and the Brain confrontation
notable_spikes: Transition from optional four-level cycle to mandatory fifth level (difficulty gate)
Boss rush in final level (cumulative skill check)
Pinky and the Brain final boss (culmination of all mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players can enter four main levels in any order before unlocking the fifth level, each containing a boss and memorabilia piece. This non-linear structure allows skill-based difficulty selection.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic 'Movie Set' level (Space Wars, Action Movie, etc.)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level is themed around different film genres (Space Opera, Action Movie, etc.), which may inform level layout and enemy types contextually
Progression requires switching between Yakko, Wakko, and Dot to use specific abilities for puzzle solving and enemy defeat; certain paths only accessible with correct character
Each level ends with a significant boss encounter requiring mastery of that level's mechanics; defeat grants memorabilia progression token
